_note: Standalone World Cup explainer cards. Use this for a static guide page, not live match coverage.

cards.title: What the World Cup actually is
cards.summary: The men's World Cup is the biggest international tournament in the sport, bringing national teams together rather than clubs.
cards.whyItMatters: This is why fans care about flags, anthems, and national identity in a way that feels different from club football.
cards.whatToWatch: The emotion is usually sharper because players may only get one or two World Cups in their whole careers.

cards.title: Why the World Cup feels bigger than normal football
cards.summary: It is rare, global, short, and emotionally compressed. Every mistake feels louder because there are fewer chances to recover.
cards.whyItMatters: That is why even all-time great players can be remembered for one tournament month.
cards.whatToWatch: Notice how conservative even star-heavy teams can become once the knockout rounds start.

cards.title: How the 2026 format works
cards.summary: The 2026 edition uses 12 groups of four teams. The top two in each group plus the eight best third-place teams advance to a Round of 32.
cards.whyItMatters: It creates more teams, more matches, and more chances for group-stage scoreboard math.
cards.whatToWatch: A team can survive one bad day and still advance, which changes the risk profile of early matches.

cards.title: The group stage is a mini league with nerves
cards.summary: Each team plays three group matches, earning points for wins and draws before the table decides who moves on.
cards.whyItMatters: This is why a draw can be useful and why goal difference becomes part of the drama.
cards.whatToWatch: Late group games can shift from wild to cautious depending on what the live table is doing.

cards.title: Third place can still be enough
cards.summary: Because 32 teams advance from 12 groups, the eight best third-place finishers also move on.
cards.whyItMatters: Teams sometimes manage a match with that escape hatch in mind instead of chasing chaos.
cards.whatToWatch: This can make the final group-round scoreboards feel interconnected across cities and kickoff windows.

cards.title: Once the bracket starts, the mood changes
cards.summary: After the group stage, the tournament becomes straight knockout football, where one bad half can end everything.
cards.whyItMatters: That pressure often makes elite teams look more careful than their talent level suggests.
cards.whatToWatch: The deeper the tournament goes, the more every goal feels like a major event instead of just part of a long season.

cards.title: A knockout match can end with penalties
cards.summary: If a knockout match is level after regulation, it can go to extra time and then a penalty shoot-out if needed.
cards.whyItMatters: A shoot-out is one of the most intense and emotionally brutal endings in all of sports.
cards.whatToWatch: The match can become more conservative late because both teams know the next mistake might decide the whole tournament.

cards.title: This is the first World Cup shared by three host countries
cards.summary: Canada, Mexico, and the United States are jointly staging the 2026 tournament.
cards.whyItMatters: It makes this edition feel especially spread out, city-driven, and continent-sized.
cards.whatToWatch: Different host cities will feel like different mini-festivals rather than one single atmosphere copied 104 times.
